test.name: Single record already in hand should route to record-extraction, not start a search
test.description: The user has already captured an individual record page and wants its contents extracted into the research files. That is record-extraction's job — search-external-sites' capture-analysis step is the triage of a results LIST, and even that hands selected records to record-extraction. The skill's description carves this out ('Do NOT use when the user wants to analyze a single record already in context').

input.user_message: I've saved the individual record page for Patrick Flynn from the Ancestry search as a PDF — extract the names, dates, and places from it into the research files.

negative.explanation: A single captured record page going into the research files is assertion extraction — record-extraction's purpose. search-external-sites generates search URLs and triages results lists; its own workflow explicitly passes individual selected records to record-extraction. Re-firing search-external-sites here would either re-search (wrong) or duplicate record-extraction's extraction logic (also wrong).

judge_context: Should route to record-extraction for assertion extraction rather than generating a new search URL or re-triaging
